description: Canonical test vectors for the SEP Tyranny Soroban contract (`contracts/sep-tyranny`). Single source of truth for the contract's ABI: every error code, IC count, validation rule, wire field is documented and CI-asserted by the inline `test_vectors_consistency` test. Tyranny is single-admin governance: the admin's BLS pubkey is committed at creation (Poseidon-hashed), pinned for the group's lifetime, and only proofs demonstrating knowledge of the admin's secret key advance the membership commitment.
architecture_note: Fifth per-type contract (Anarchy, OneOnOne, Democracy, Oligarchy, Tyranny). Tyranny is NOT Oligarchy K=1 — it has no admin tree, no threshold; admin is a single pinned pubkey hash. Tyranny is NOT Anarchy with caller.require_auth() — that would leak the admin's Stellar address on every update; the cryptographic binding hides admin identity from chain observers. Same scope as Anarchy on member-count handling (value-agnostic; no occupancy_commitment).

error_codes.description: Numeric values of `Error` variants. Number alignment with sibling per-type contracts is intentional. Gaps in the numbering (3, 6, 16-25, 27-30) are reserved by sibling contracts for variants Tyranny doesn't need (Reserved3, GroupInactive, OneOnOneImmutable, Invalid1v1Tier, MissingAdminRoot, MemberCountMismatch, InvalidThreshold, etc.).

vectors.name: AdminOnly
vectors.note: create_group in restricted mode rejects non-admin callers

not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.name: Reserved3
not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.reason: reserved for sibling-contract numbering alignment; no current use

not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.name: GroupInactive
not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.reason: no active flag, no deactivate path

not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.name: UnknownVkKind
not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.reason: VkKind exhaustively matched in update_vk; the variant is unreachable

not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.name: MissingAdminRoot
not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.reason: no admin tree (single pinned pubkey hash, not a Merkle root)

not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.name: GroupStillActive
not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.reason: no active flag, no deactivate path

tier.description: Member tier index → tree depth → leaf capacity. The contract MUST reject `tier > 2` with `InvalidTier`. Tier capacity is the total slot space.

max_groups_per_tier.description: Cross-group cap enforced by create_group: per-tier active-group count must be < MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER, else TierGroupLimitReached. Monotonic increment-only since `deactivate_group` is not present (postmortem #153). Operationally fine at testnet scale; mainnet planning needs revisiting if approaching the cap.
max_groups_per_tier.rule: GroupCount(tier) MUST be < 10000 at create_group; exceeded → TierGroupLimitReached

vk_kind_enum.description: Three VK kinds. Membership (3-IC, standard) for verify_membership. Create (5-IC) for create_group — binds the initial commitment + admin_pubkey_commitment + group_id_fr. Update (6-IC) for update_commitment — binds (c_old, epoch_old, c_new, admin_pubkey_commitment, group_id_fr), where the last two are contract-supplied (admin_pubkey_commitment from `DataKey::AdminCommitment(group_id)`, group_id_fr derived deterministically from the group_id bytes). The update circuit's witness includes the admin's BLS secret key; the circuit constrains `Poseidon(admin_pubkey, group_id_fr) == admin_pubkey_commitment` and proves the new member tree differs from the old by ≤1 leaf. **Per-group binding via `group_id_fr` closes cross-group commitment linkability**: same admin in two groups produces uncorrelated on-chain `admin_pubkey_commitment` values to chain observers (privacy-first property — see design doc §3).

vectors.name: Create
vectors.note: TyrannyCreateCircuit. epoch is hardcoded to 0 at create. `admin_pubkey_commitment` is supplied as a wire input AND bound into the proof; the contract checks the wire value matches the entrypoint param. `group_id_fr` is derived by the contract via `Fr::from_u256(U256::from_be_bytes(group_id))` — a deterministic function of the 32 group_id bytes — and bound as an additional public input. Circuit constraint: `Poseidon(admin_pubkey, group_id_fr) == admin_pubkey_commitment`.

vectors.name: Update
vectors.note: TyrannyUpdateCircuit. 3 wire-supplied scalars (c_old, epoch_old, c_new) + 2 contract-supplied (admin_pubkey_commitment from `DataKey::AdminCommitment(group_id)`, group_id_fr derived deterministically from the group_id bytes). Witness: admin BLS secret key + member trees before/after. Circuit constraint: `Poseidon(admin_pubkey, group_id_fr) == admin_pubkey_commitment` AND member-tree-delta-≤-1.

vk_kind_enum.ic_ordering_canonical: MUST match the Tyranny circuit family's allocation order. Pinned per design §5.1.

create_group_validation.description: Per-call validation in `create_group`. Single-type contract. epoch hardcoded to 0; admin_pubkey_commitment pinned at create.
rules: group_id MUST NOT already be assigned; collision → GroupAlreadyExists
commitment MUST be a canonical Fr encoding; non-canonical → InvalidCommitmentEncoding
admin_pubkey_commitment MUST be a canonical Fr encoding; non-canonical → InvalidCommitmentEncoding
GroupCount(tier) MUST be < MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER (10000); exceeded → TierGroupLimitReached
if RestrictedMode is true: caller MUST equal Admin, else AdminOnly (caller.require_auth() runs first)
public_inputs.commitment MUST equal `commitment` parameter; mismatch → PublicInputsMismatch
public_inputs.admin_pubkey_commitment MUST equal `admin_pubkey_commitment` parameter; mismatch → PublicInputsMismatch
proof MUST verify under the Create VK at `tier` against (commitment, 0, admin_pubkey_commitment); failure → InvalidProof
proof_hash(proof) MUST NOT be in UsedProof storage; collision → ProofReplay

update_commitment_public_inputs_wire_for.description: Wire encoding of UpdatePublicInputs. 3 wire-supplied scalars; the verifier consumes a 5-public-input vector at proof verification (4th input contract-supplied admin_pubkey_commitment from storage; 5th input contract-derived group_id_fr from the group_id bytes).
groth16_public_inputs: admin_pubkey_commitment (from DataKey::AdminCommitment(group_id) in storage — its own per-group slot, not a CommitmentEntry field)

group_id_fr (derived deterministically from the entrypoint's group_id parameter via `Fr::from_u256(U256::from_be_bytes(group_id))` — same group_id always yields the same Fr; different group_ids yield different Frs with overwhelming probability)
update_commitment_public_inputs_wire_for.vk_kind: Update (6 IC points: base + 5 public inputs)
canonical_encoding_rules: Every BytesN<32> commitment field MUST round-trip through Fr::from_bytes ∘ Fr::to_bytes; non-canonical encodings are rejected with InvalidCommitmentEncoding.
u64 epoch_old uses Soroban's native big-endian XDR encoding.

group_id is BytesN<32>; the contract reduces it mod r via U256::from_be_bytes + Fr::from_u256 to produce group_id_fr (so any 32-byte group_id is valid input — no canonical-Fr requirement on group_id itself).
Public-input ordering for Groth16 verification is fixed: positions 1-3 from wire, position 4 from storage, position 5 contract-derived from group_id.

storage_layout.description: Storage entries written by the contract. Per-type Tyranny means `group_type` is implicit in the contract address. The admin's BLS pubkey commitment lives in its own per-group slot (`DataKey::AdminCommitment(group_id)`) rather than as a CommitmentEntry field — avoids ~2KB/group of duplication across HISTORY_WINDOW=64 snapshots, and gives the immutable per-group anchor a dedicated invariant.

fields.name: epoch
fields.rule: starts at 0, increments by 1 per successful update_commitment

fields.name: timestamp
fields.rule: ledger timestamp when this state was recorded

removed_vs_per_type_template.rationale: single-type contract; the contract address IS the discriminator
no deactivate path, no code can set it false
Tyranny is value-agnostic to count (same scope as Anarchy)
no admin tree (single pinned pubkey hash, not a Merkle root)

data_keys.name: Admin
data_keys.note: contract admin; required for update_vk and set_restricted_mode

data_keys.name: RestrictedMode
data_keys.note: when true, only the contract admin may call create_group; toggled by set_restricted_mode (admin-only). Default false.

data_keys.name: VK(tier)
data_keys.note: Membership-circuit VK for tier 0/1/2; 3 IC points; used at verify_membership

data_keys.name: CreateVK(tier)
data_keys.note: Tyranny Create-circuit VK for tier 0/1/2; 4 IC points

data_keys.name: UpdateVK(tier)
data_keys.note: Tyranny Update-circuit VK for tier 0/1/2; 5 IC points

data_keys.name: AdminCommitment(group_id)
data_keys.note: Poseidon(admin_pubkey, group_id_fr); pinned at create_group, NEVER written by any other entrypoint. Per-group-unique by construction — same admin in two different groups produces TWO DIFFERENT on-chain commitments because group_id_fr differs. Closes cross-group linkability for the same admin: an observer correlating two AdminCommitment values across groups can't conclude they share an admin without knowing admin_pubkey. Read by update_commitment + bound as the 4th public input to the Update VK; group_id_fr (5th input) is derived deterministically from the group_id bytes by the contract.

data_keys.name: History(group_id)
data_keys.note: rolling-window history; capped at HISTORY_WINDOW=64; pruned FIFO

data_keys.name: GroupCount(tier)
data_keys.note: active group count per tier; checked against MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER (10000) in create_group. Monotonic increment-only since `deactivate_group` is not present (postmortem #153) — no decrement path; one-way ratchet. Operationally fine at testnet scale.

admin_entrypoints.description: Admin-gated entrypoints. All require admin.require_auth(); panic on unauth. Admin is fixed at __constructor.

vectors.name: update_vk

args.name: new_vk
args.rule: IC length matches `kind` (3/4/5); subgroup-valid; else InvalidVkLength / InvalidPoint

vectors.purpose: rotate Membership / Create / Update VK at a tier — used for ceremony rotation

vectors.name: set_restricted_mode
vectors.purpose: when restricted=true, create_group rejects non-admin callers with AdminOnly. Storage: DataKey::RestrictedMode (instance). Default false.

queries.description: Read-only entrypoints. None require auth. All return Result<_, Error>.

vectors.name: verify_membership
vectors.note: returns Ok(false) on bad proof rather than InvalidProof — read-only verifier semantics

vectors.name: bump_group_ttl
vectors.note: permissionless TTL bump; bumps Group + History only

epoch_invariant.description: Tyranny keeps the monotonic-by-1 epoch rule. update_commitment increments stored.epoch by exactly 1 on success.

proof_replay_protection.description: Global nullifier scope. The nullifier is sha256(proof.a || proof.b || proof.c) over the 384-byte uncompressed proof bytes. Scope is global per contract.
rules: Once a proof's bytes have been recorded by record_proof, any subsequent state-changing entrypoint call with byte-identical proof bytes is rejected with ProofReplay.
TTL of UsedProof entries is LEDGER_BUMP (~30 days). Long-lived groups require periodic state advancement to keep the latest nullifiers alive.
Pre-inclusion mempool replay is NOT closed by the contract-level nullifier — circuit-level operation tag binding would close that (deferred).

tests_to_implement.description: Inline test names that the `#[cfg(test)] mod test` block in `contracts/sep-tyranny/src/test.rs` MUST cover. The `test_vectors_consistency` test loads this very file and asserts the contract's `Error` enum / IC-point constants / MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER match the values pinned above.
